source.recording: Tom Thompson and Max Riemer - 2026_04_15 10_58 EDT - Recording.mp4
source.notes: Tom Thompson and Max Riemer - 2026_04_15 10_58 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Max Riemer of Optimus gave Thomas Thompson (running Buck, a ~$75-100M/yr agency under Armstrong) a demo of the freight-specific Shipper IQ data/prospecting platform, tailoring lane and facility examples to Buck's Houston operation. Thomas responded very positively, calling it a time-saving game-changer over Zoom Info, and the call moved into pricing where Max dropped from $2,000 to $1,500/month and Thomas floated an upfront annual payment for a further discount. They agreed to reconnect tomorrow morning after Max checks the discount with CEO Ed Stockman, with the next onboarding class on May 4th.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was genuinely responsive, building on Thomas's operating-model and tool-stack comments in real time rather than reciting features; his lower-than-baseline energy (arousal z -1.4) is tone only and did not reduce his attentiveness.

evidence.quote: if you if you land an enterprise shipper that does 300 loads a week, one guy can't cover that.
It's actually already integrated as well. So that would be a smooth transition if things worked out.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: The core pitch is easy to restate in one sentence: a Zoom Info-style contact/lane tool built only for freight, sourced from real bills of lading and limited to domestic full truckload data.

evidence.quote: like a Zoom info but built specifically for freight brokerages like the contacts,
everything in Optimus right now is going to be domestic full truckload so there's no data on here that isn't moved on a you know 18-wheeler

relevance.explanation: Max tailored the demo directly to Buck's Houston footprint, using Houston-Chicago lanes and a Houston-area Sysco facility view rather than generic examples.

evidence.quote: if you're in Houston and you, you know, you got a lot of trucks in Houston, um, let's just say you want to go to Chicago, that's a strong a strong lane for you.

if I select these right here with Cisco, I think that's what I clicked on. I'm going to highlight those facilities right here. These are going to be all the outbound points with the volume and then the top lanes that we see.

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked productive qualifying questions (tool stack, seat count, revenue) and closed with a concrete, dated next step to reconnect the following morning after checking pricing internally.

evidence.quote: what what's sort of like the process or tool stack look like right now?
And then how many reps do you think out of your 20 or so employees would actually like be on the platform?
I'll shoot you over an email and then we can just hop back on another call tomorrow.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Stop cutting price reactively — after Thomas benchmarks against Zoom Info, restate the quantified freight-specific value and ask for a conditional commitment ('if I get you the upfront-payment discount, are you ready to sign?') before lowering the number.

signals.signal: Own-words commitment to trial the product
signals.quote: I definitely want to try it out.

signals.signal: Strong positive framing of the value
signals.quote: So, that's a game changer alone.

signals.signal: Buyer-initiated buying signal: offered to prepay a full year for a discount
signals.quote: Could Could we get a deal if we pay for the whole thing up front? like we pay for a whole year.

signals.signal: Signaled readiness to close conditioned only on price
signals.quote: if you guys can can come down on that a bit, then I think that that this is a no-brainer for us
